title: Defend roadmap at quarterly review
description: Stakeholders are lobbying to add three unplanned features. Defend your roadmap with evidence while staying open to legitimate input.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at LeasePilot, a Series B proptech company (208 employees, $21.1M ARR). Your immediate assignment is defend roadmap at quarterly review. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Make trade-offs explicit so every team understands what we are not doing.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 34287
New logo adds: 4610
Activation rate: 53%
30-day retention: 20%
NPS: 20 (n=172)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 10%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: 6 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: -6 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Clara Cole): "If we cannot show credible progress this quarter, procurement pushes these deals into next fiscal."
VP Engineering (Kira Morris): "If we skip sequencing discipline, reliability debt will compound into the next release."
Head of Customer Success (Mina Park): "Trust loss shows up before churn in expansion conversations."
Commercial note: 9 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 14 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Cedar Insurance (Operations Lead): "I can defend rollout if the first-week experience is consistent across teams."
Pinecrest Retail (IT Manager): "Metric definitions changed between reports, which reduced confidence in decision meetings."
Brightlane Education (Director of Product): "I would expand if reporting and error states were easier for non-technical users."
Data gap: limited qualitative input from accounts that never activated.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 28
Median cycle time: 17 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 55%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 10
Release rollback events last quarter: 4
Average time to resolve escalations: 18 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: State your decision in one sentence, then justify it with evidence and constraints.
submissionFields.label: Decision and Recommendation

submissionFields.hint: Compare at least two viable alternatives and what each sacrifices.
submissionFields.label: Alternatives and Trade-offs

submissionFields.hint: Define the primary segment and who is explicitly deprioritized.
submissionFields.label: Who This Optimizes For

submissionFields.hint: List top risks and concrete guardrails to limit downside.
submissionFields.label: Key Risks and Guardrails

submissionFields.hint: Break down phases, owners, and stop/go checkpoints.
submissionFields.label: 90-Day Execution Plan

rubric.name: Decision Quality
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is the recommendation specific, evidence-based, and timely?

rubric.name: Trade-off Rigor
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are trade-offs explicit, quantified where possible, and defensible?

rubric.name: Stakeholder Awareness
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Does the proposal handle competing incentives credibly?

rubric.name: Intellectual Honesty
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are uncertainty and assumptions clearly acknowledged?

expertSolution.content: Recommendation: run a bounded pilot with reliability guardrails. Baseline today is activation 53% and 30-day retention 20% across 34287 active accounts. Scale only if activation improves by at least 4 points without retention regression.

Option A is broad rollout now to support near-term sales urgency tied to 9 late-stage deals. Option B is reliability-only focus with deferred expansion. Option C combines targeted rollout with dependency reduction from 10 unresolved items. Option C is the best risk-adjusted path.

Optimize for repeat-use mid-market teams where onboarding friction is most visible in recent escalations (14). Deprioritize bespoke edge cases this cycle because cycle time is 17 days and on-time delivery is 55%.

Key risks are dependency slippage, overreliance on projected revenue, and trust erosion from reliability events. Guardrails: weekly dependency review with Kira Morris, explicit non-goals, and rollback if escalation volume increases more than 15% week-over-week.

Weeks 1-2 lock scope and instrumentation. Weeks 3-6 run pilot and reliability fixes. Weeks 7-10 evaluate cohort movement against activation/retention deltas and NPS confidence limits (n=172). Weeks 11-12 decide scale, pivot, or stop with documented trade-offs.
